reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Japanese, preserving code blocks, MDX components, frontmatter, etc. Must keep frontmatter keys order, translate only reader-facing values: title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit (none present). Also language field? Not present; we need to add language? The spec says if a language field exists, set it to "Japanese". Not required to add if not present. So keep frontmatter as is, but translate title and subTitle values. Also maybe need to add language field? Not required. Keep other keys unchanged.

We must translate the prose, preserving headings levels. Also translate blockquote text. Keep code unchanged. Also need to prefix relative URLs with ../. The original markdown includes image URLs like ./hero-wide.webp etc. They are relative; we need to prefix with ../. So change ./hero-wide.webp to ../hero-wide.webp? Actually spec: "ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`." So for each relative URL, add ../ before. For example cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp becomes ../hero-wide.webp. Similarly cover_mobile, cover_icon. Also inside markdown content: image ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) -> change to ../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg. Also ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) -> ../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. Also the blockquote link: [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/) is absolute, keep unchanged.

Also any other relative URLs? None.

Now translate the text. Keep code blocks unchanged. Keep blockquote tags and class attributes unchanged, but translate inner text.

Also there is a comment {/* ¹ Yes, I know that `URI` is more correct than `URL`. If you are pedantic enough to actually care about that distinction, please go touch grass. */}. This is a JSX comment; we need to keep as is, but translate the comment text? It's a comment, not displayed. It's reader-facing? It's inside comment, but likely not displayed. The spec says translate only reader-facing text. This comment is not displayed, but it's part of source. Might be considered not displayed, but it's still text. Probably keep as is, but we could translate? Safer to keep unchanged because it's code comment. We'll keep unchanged.

Now produce translation.

Let's translate title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" -> "llm:// 接続文字列の時代が来た". subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" -> "`llm://` URL でモデルとプロバイダーの設定を簡素化". Keep backticks.

Now translate body.

First blockquote: "**Update:** This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](...).". Translate: "**更新:** 本記事は `llm://` URI スキームの [Internet-Draft](...) に繋がりました。" Keep bold.

Then the paragraph about bad old days. Translate.

We need to keep tone technical, direct.

Let's craft translation.

Will preserve headings: ## The Env Var Explosion etc. Translate headings.

Heading "The Env Var Explosion" -> "環境変数の氾濫". "Introducing LLM Connection Strings" -> "LLM 接続文字列の紹介". "Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" -> "LLM 接続文字列の構成要素". "Need auth? Great, add it." -> "認証が必要ですか？ では追加しましょう。". "Resiliency? Why the hell not." -> "レジリエンス？ なぜだめなのか。". "Alternative Formats" -> "代替フォーマット". "The Verdict" -> "結論". Also blockquote with class "inset" after llms://: "One string with everything from your **auth** to your **endpoint** to your **hyperparameters**." Translate: "認証からエンドポイント、ハイパーパラメータまで、すべてを1つの文字列で。" Keep ** for auth, endpoint, hyperparameters? They are bold. Keep bold.

Also blockquote class "ai-response inset": "The database world took decades to figure this out.\nGood news, in AI timelines, that's only about half a vibe-year ago." Translate: "データベース界がこれを実現するまでに何十年もかかりました。\n良いニュースは、AI のタイムラインではそれがたった半年ほど前のことです。" Keep <b> for Good news line.

Now translate paragraphs.

Let's write translation.

Be careful with code block comments: the comment includes footnote marker ¹. Keep unchanged.

Now produce final output.
